title: leontodon borealis
intro: Leontodon borealis, also known as the Arctic hawk's beard or Arctic leontodon, is a species of flowering plant in the dandelion family that is native to the Arctic tundra. The plant's roots, leaves, and flowers have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Leontodon borealis include its ability to reduce fever, treat digestive issues, and alleviate respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and coughs. The plant can be prepared as an infusion, tincture, or decoction, with the roots being used more frequently in herbal preparations due to their higher concentration of bioactive compounds.
